newsSinceJun6: No new fundamental headlines since June 6. The -7.0% move on June 9 extends a momentum/valuation reset that began June 5 (-8%) after the stock set a 52-week high near $474 on June 2; coverage attributes the slide to profit-taking, premium-to-intrinsic-value flags, ~$1.4M of insider selling over three months, and analyst price-target trims with ratings maintained - explicitly not a single fundamental headline. Thesis-relevant fundamentals (raised FY26 revenue guide ~$19B; 1.6T CPO Ethernet switch hyperscaler win ramping 2027) date to the April 27 Q1 release and are unchanged. Judged flow-driven, thesis intact; stock is now roughly -24% off its June 2 peak with YTD compressed to +18.9% from ~+50% at the recent high.

sources.label: Quiver Quant - Celestica shares slide as momentum cools after record run-up (Jun 5, 2026)
Yahoo Finance - Celestica (CLS) Suffers a Larger Drop Than the General Market
Yahoo Finance / Simply Wall St - Does Celestica's Insider Selling Undermine Analyst Optimism?
Celestica Q1 2026 results press release (Apr 27, 2026; CPO 1.6T switch win, raised FY26 guide)

verdicts.rankRationale: This page parked CLS at 94 specifically for post-rerating crowding; a flow-driven ~24% drawdown off the June 2 peak with the Helios/1.6T thesis intact restores some residual alpha and asymmetry, warranting a modest move up from the bottom of the list.
verdicts.bull: Direct rack-scale AI and 1.6T CPO switch bottleneck exposure now available ~24% cheaper than a week ago, with FY26 guidance recently raised and no negative fundamental news driving the reset.
verdicts.neutral: The name remains fully discovered and heavily covered; the drawdown improves entry but does not restore the 'hidden' character this alpha list rewards.
verdicts.bear: Momentum unwinds in crowded AI EMS names can run far past fair value, and insider selling plus premium-valuation flags suggest the reset may not be finished.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Q2 2026 results and any new hyperscaler HPS/CPO program awards confirming the 2027 ramp.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Continued multiple compression as the AI-hardware momentum trade unwinds, independent of execution.

verdicts.rankRationale: Hold: this page penalizes CLS for being a discovered trade, and a price drawdown does not make it more latent - discovery is the structural critique and it stands; yesterday's down move already reflected the adjusted score, and there is no new information today.
verdicts.bull: Connectivity & services exposure (800G/1.6T switching, hyperscaler optics) is real and deepening, and the post-peak reset cheapens a high-quality node.
verdicts.neutral: Flow-driven selloff with thesis intact slightly improves the setup, but Celestica's AI role is fully mapped by the market, which is exactly what caps it on a latent-nodes list.
verdicts.bear: Hyperscaler concentration means any program rebalancing or insourcing decision hits hard, and the stock still trades on AI narrative rather than undiscovered fundamentals.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: New program disclosures broadening the customer base beyond the current hyperscaler concentration.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Customer concentration plus a crowded shareholder base leaves no latency premium to harvest.

verdicts.rankRationale: Yesterday's down move was premised on a ~50% YTD rerating that has now largely unwound (YTD +18.9%, ~24% off the June 2 peak) on flows, not fundamentals; with the deployment/systems-integration thesis intact and FY26 guidance raised, asymmetry has genuinely improved on this page's alpha lens.
verdicts.bull: Rack integration and data-center systems exposure with a raised $19B FY26 revenue guide and a 2027 CPO switch ramp, bought after a one-week, news-free 24% reset.
verdicts.neutral: Execution and mix are unchanged; the move only resets valuation toward where the page already scored the fundamentals, so the score change is modest.
verdicts.bear: EMS margins are thin and cyclical, and if hyperscaler capex digestion arrives in 2H26 the guidance that anchors the thesis becomes the risk.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Q2 2026 print validating the $19B guide and HPS margin trajectory.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Hyperscaler capex deceleration or program loss compressing both estimates and the multiple simultaneously.
